_description: Standard numerical accuracy tiers for all molresponse_v3 tests. These settings define the DFT block defaults and are shared across all systems and properties regardless of xc functional.
_note_on_protocol: The effective dconv in SCF is max(thresh, user_dconv) — see SCF.cc:2143. The final protocol value must be <= dconv to achieve the desired density accuracy. k is auto-selected from thresh: 1e-2→4, 1e-4→6, 1e-6→8, 1e-8→10.
_note_on_L: L should be at least 10x the spatial extent of the system. 200.0 is safe for most molecules up to naphthalene. Small systems (H2, He, Li) can use smaller L.
_note_on_eprec: Nuclear potential smoothing. Must match the target accuracy tier — too coarse introduces geometry-dependent errors.

low.description: Fast, fuzzy calculations for smoke tests and development iteration
low.use_case: CI smoke tests, development cycle, quick validation

medium.description: Standard production accuracy — default for most tests and property validation
medium.use_case: Property validation, v2/v3 parity checks, reference value generation

high.description: High accuracy for protocol convergence studies and publication-quality results
high.use_case: Protocol convergence studies, basis-set-limit benchmarks, Dalton comparisons

common.xc_note: HF is the default for all tiers. Override xc to test LDA, PBE, etc. — numerical settings stay the same.
